event.name: RKS_STORE_SNIPER_SIEGED
event.text: Looks like an EXTREME-ly deadly stealth ship is attacking the nearby market owned by a certain "Sniper Girl". If you can stop them, you should be able to access the store. The specialized store looks ready to provide fire support.
You prepare for combat.
You are pretty sure they noticed your presence, but they seem too busy to care.

destroyed.text: The stealth ship breaks apart and you quickly salvage what you can.

deadCrew.text: With the crew dead you quickly salvage what you can from the stealth ship.

event.name: RKS_STORE_SNIPER_WIN
event.text:
Sniper Girl wastes no time hailing you. "They just keep coming, with better equipment every time, to rob us. I just can't catch a breath. I know that's why you're here too..." You're about to say something to cheer her up when she cuts you off. "Because there's NO better shop in this universe for Stealth gear! So efficient, pirates dream about it, coalitions plot to acquire it, and renegades jump in to steal it! Buy while you still can!". Turns out she's still in character. RK sector professionals must have seen some shit, you think to yourself.
"...And for your timely assistance, here's a little incentive to buy." You realize the gift is your "Thank you", and shrug. Then you take one look at the wares and immediately understand why the attacker came here.
"Oh and, some of this stuff is so hard to source it shouldn't even exist, so only 1 purchase per customer. But for you I'll make it 3."

event.name: RKS_FIGHT_ELITE_COALITION
event.text: The elite Coalition ship here is flying away from the fresh wreck of an unlucky smuggler. They sound very excited on the open comms. "Ok we have steal good weapons. Now, must test! I see prey here, gogogo!"
"Go find a different ship to test that on, fools!" "Oh shit, Nights on that ship? Tkkkktk. Power engines, must find preyyyy!"
"Go find a different ship to test that on, please!" "Oh shit, Tyrdeo on that ship? Tkkkktk. Power engines, must find preyyyy!"
You prepare for combat.

event.name: RKS_SUSPICIOUS_SMUGGLER
event.text: The ship hiding at this hazardous beacon has no conspicuous pirate markings, but behaves very strangely. You suspect it's a smuggler. Fighting it will certainly destroy whatever cargo they're hiding.

choice.text: Without a Police Warrant, you can't submit them to a search.

event.text:
"Oh god dammit, you're with those Zoltan party poopers? Just my luck... I'm not risking our lives over this, come on in."
After a thorough search, you find their RK augment! You're supposed to drop it off at the Police Station marked on your map, but keeping it could help with your more important mission.

"Oh god dammit, you're with those Zoltan party poopers? Just my luck... I'm not risking our lives over this, come on in."
After a thorough search, you find their RK weaponry! You're supposed to drop it off at the Police Station marked on your map, but keeping it could help with your more important mission.
They immediately start powering their weapons!
You prepare for combat.
You don't approach, but the ship seems aware that you noticed them. "Hey, uh, what are you looking at?"
"Y-yeah? And what about it?"
"O-oh. Yeah, of course. Come aboard!"
You prepare for combat.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_DEFROMORIUS
event.text:
The distress beacon is coming from a large Elite Harmony ship, but it's already starting to blow up! Scans don't seem to reveal any life signs inside, so it's not like you could have saved anyone.
There might be just enough time to catch the black box if you have a quick solution.

choice.text: A Temporal system could have helped here.
(Temporal Manipulator) Slow down the black box's area and retrieve it.

event.text: Your Temporal Manipulator allows you to retrieve the box comfortably. You start scrapping the wreck during decoding.

choice.text: With good Sensors and some Hacking we could have decoded it.

event.text: It takes but a moment for your hacking drone to reach the black box and find out what happened. You also start scrapping the wreck.

choice.text: Your Engi locates the box and says your Lanius could catch it before it's too late.

event.text:
"Suggestion: throw metalvore through airlock in less than 8.7534 seconds. Chance of success = significant." You're not too sure about this, until your Lanius happily jumps out to grab the box like a kid chasing a tasty snack!
Fortunately it's used to this type of acrobatics, and avoids any injuries while also bringing back some scrap. It adds, "Ship tasted weird. Bit familiar."

choice.text: There's nothing we can do, just scrap whatever's left.

event.text: You wait for the ship to finish blowing up, then enjoy the free scrap.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_DEFROMORIUS_2
event.text:
According to the box, the crew were crushed or impaled, and the ship sabotaged, save for the Life Support and Medbay. A single shuttle managed to escape before the ship blew up. Another ship was present, maybe the ones who did this. You realize it's still here, docked not far from this beacon!
Close enough to see what's up before jumping away.
You easily catch up to them, and they haven't noticed you yet.

"We were selling mind torture devices to Engi Defenders. The deal went sour went something in their ship went on a rampage. A shuttle escaped. We ran."
That solves the mystery. You also extract the shuttle's destination and leave like nothing happened.

"The Feds are here for revenge! I told you we should have jumped away, Lisa."
"But why? We're innocent! Why else would we still be here, just tell them that you numbnut!"
"Hah! Like they would believe us, why are you always so naive? Charge the jump drive already!"
If you really think they could be innocent, you'll have to rough up their hull without killing them to get answers.

choice.text: No thanks, finish scrapping then we're leaving.

event.text: Why take risks when there's scrap right here and more important things to do?

gotaway.text: As the ship escapes, you ask around the dock but nobody tells you anything. There goes your only lead in this mystery. Back to the mission.

surrender.text:
"It wasn't us! I beg you in the name of Lisa, stop! We'll tell you everything!"
"Wait, what does that have to do with me? They don't even kno-... Oh, whatever."

event.text:
"We were just minding our own business, on the way to this dock to sell our trinkets..." As you dig into their eyes with your hand ready to gesture "Fire at will", they adjust their story.
"Ok mayyyybe we were selling a little neural shocker or two to those Engis off the books. But then they started acting strange and sent a Distress signal. Being just humble merchants, we took it as a clue to get the hell away! Right, Lisa?"
"Just tell him about the shuttle, numbnut! No crazy stuff, just the facts."
"Oh yeah, right! We detected this small vessel escaping, and scans said there was this big monster inside! Some kind of Frankenstein! You know what, that must be the killer right there, it was an inside job I tell you!" They start rambling about various conspiracy theories while "Lisa" in the back looks increasingly worried about their mental health.

choice.text: We believe you. Where did the shuttle go?

event.text: As a security measure they had trackers in their merchandise. That gives you the shuttle's destination.

choice.text: That's probably a lie, keep fighting!

destroyed.text: The ship breaks apart and you salvage what you can. Maybe justice was served, you can't really be sure.

choice.text: Even if it wasn't them, scrap is scrap.

deadCrew.text: With the crew dead you salvage what you can. Maybe justice was served, you can't really be sure.

choice.text: Even if it wasn't them, scrap is scrap.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_DEFROMORIUS_SHUTTLE

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The destination of the creature likely responsible for goring the Engis.

event.text:
You quickly find the shuttle, which didn't get very far since it turns out it took serious damage at some point. Scans indeed reveal life inside, slowly fading. You detect markers of Engi Defender... and Lanius?... and something else too??
That doesn't make sense since there's only 1 creature detected.

To your surprise, it does seem to be some sort of Engi Defender, and it definitely answers you like one.
"Self = in jeopardy but know you = Federation. --COMPUTING-- This beacon = not likely your mission. Your presence here: purpose?"

choice.text: Did you kill the Engi Defenders?

event.text:
"Affirmative. Number of reasons to lie = 0. Self = anomaly. --PAIN-- Accidental discovery: Lanius metalflesh: can bind to Engi nanites. Requirements: matter from Shell forbidden experiments + extremely unlikely radiation burst. --STATISTICALLY IMPOSSIBLE-- Harmony verdict: undo or eliminate."
You don't seem very convinced, but the creature keeps going.
"Solution for survival: join the RadiKals. --OBJECTIVE COMPLETE-- However, Harmony = persistent. Captured me. Attempted to reset with: neural torture. Solution for escape: kill. --OBJECTIVE COMPLETE-- Now: your intents?"

choice.text: We could protect you from the Harmony.

event.text:
"Answer = unexpected. You = fools: 97% probability. Regardless, I cease functioning in: [time.short]"
The creature does look desperate for oxygen by now. You can't risk bringing it in just yet, but maybe you can help it remotely and see what happens.

choice.text: You don't have ways to sufficiently oxygenize outside of your ship.
You can't spread Medbay healing around.
Leave it to die, it did deserve it.

event.text: You wait for the creature to suffocate, thrashing around the ship as it does so, then scrap the remains of the shuttle.

"Outcome: expected. Solutions = none. Proceed, this = better than: lethal suffocation."
You safely put the creature out of its misery from a distance, then scrap the remains of the shuttle.

choice.text: This cannot end well, better leave now.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_DEFROMORIUS_OFFER
event.text:
"Wow. This solution: ingenious. You = promising, now 51% probability. --NEW OBJECTIVE: JOIN YOU--"
Looks like you have new loyal crew. You ask their name.
"Self ID = 'RK (Engi) Defender x Rockman-shaped-Morph x Lanius [amalgam]'. Else If: you = limited brainpower, use alias 'Def_Rock-Morph_Lanius'."
You decide 'Defromorius' will do. Once healed, it helps you fully scrap the shuttle and you're all ready to go.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_BAM

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: You were told you can get great RadiKal Bam gear at this beacon, if you can prove you're a customer.

event.text:
The planet here is surrounded by majestic, profitable, and highly hazardous asteroid rings, but you're far enough not to risk collisions. The longer and farther away you scan, the more auto-ships you detect harvesting minerals from them. Plenty of manned ships are also practicing flight maneuvers, fighting each other in what seems to be friendly sparring, or patrolling. Some of those seem to have noticed you but haven't reacted so far.
Now a hail is coming from the planet, which you take. "Enjoying the view, Captain? Instead of just sitting there, show us you're a customer or get lost."

choice.text: You do not have any RK Bam gear.

event.text: "... Homie! That's what I'm talking about. Here's the welcome gift. Hurry up and come over so we can trade!"

choice.text: You do not have any RK gear at all.

event.text: "Nice. Hope you're bringing anything for us. We have gear. Hurry up and come over so we can trade!"

choice.text: (Non-Unique Mantis) We have 'workpieces' for sale, could you direct us to the right place?

event.text: "Righto, then it's the Punk lab you want. I can send you there instead."
"Tell them they stink while you're at it. Hah! Those smug freaks."

choice.text: We already have that lab's coords, but thanks.
(Non-Unique Orchids) We have 'flammable material' for sale, could you direct us to the right place?

event.text: "Righto, then it's the Scorch lab you want. I can send you there instead."
"Tell them they stink while you're at it. Hah! That's gonna make their blood boil."

choice.text: We already have that lab's coords, but thanks.

event.text: Your ship soon takes damage from a source you cannot identify. "F**k off, tourist. You had been warned."

choice.text: We're just passing through here, don't mind us.

event.text:
There is no answer. You move a safe distance away and figure you have time to do some maintenance.
After some time, you detect some Shell ships jumping in weapons hot, but you're safe here.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_BAM_2
event.text:
"So, what've you got? Anything hard, or explosive? And breathing? Or even inanimate, that's fine too, we're not like the Scorch." He says that like they're so much better, but you don't think it's a good idea to point that out.
You look at what they want and get a better understanding of why this sector is outlawed by so many factions. However the mission comes first, right?

choice.text: You don't have Shells for this (Uniques would rather kill you and themselves)

event.text:
"Explodey ball, always nice. Here's your cash."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Explodey-er ball! Here's big cash!"
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Insta-heal ball! Here's big cash!"
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Insta-repair ball! Perfect for A.E.R.A., here's big cash!"
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Multi-explodey ball! You brought a rare treat, here's super cash!!"
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

choice.text: Actually... we have an emergency, bye!

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_BAM_3
event.text:
"OK, now enjoy our best local store. I've got shit to take care of, ya know? Sounds kinda urgent. Bye."
Before you shop, you still have some time to do something else aboard the ship.

event.name: RKS_LAB_BAM_AMBUSH
event.text:
You barely even reach the Jump button as a small Shell fleet jumps in! A very angry Radiant ship immediately hails you.
"Federation simpletons, don't get me wrong, these guys actually do cool science, and we even know a few of our rogue elites are high-ranking in there."
For a brief moment, you think this is gonna end peacefully.
"But it's at our expense, plus they make a lot of breaches, like, A LOT! It's crazy. Anyway our management just can't tolerate all this anymore and unfortunately letting you just fly out of there alive would look bad for us."
You wonder why they would expose themselves to weaponry so specifically good against their biology, but your scans reveal they came quite prepared!

choice.text: (Alkali) Speaking of, make way idiots. Secret mission.

event.text:
"Ah shit, here we go again. The Geniocracy is back, pals. You know what to do: EXPLOSIONS!"
Projectiles from the RK Bam and the assault fleet start flying in every direction! Also, they must have used a special jammer to depower your jump drive, you will have to charge it again.

destroyed.text: The Elite Shell ship breaks apart and you quickly salvage what you can before the rest of the fleet realizes what happened.

choice.text: The Shells are messed up, but we might be worse.

deadCrew.text: With the crew dead you quickly salvage what you can from the Elite Shell ship before the rest of the fleet realizes what happened.

choice.text: The Shells are messed up, but we might be worse.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_PUNK

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: You were told you can get great RadiKal Punk gear at this beacon, if you can prove you're a customer.

event.text:
So much blue. Not an island in sight, this planet is covered in water. And when you look closely, a few floating structures. A few hovering ones too. Deeper scans can only penetrate the endless ocean so much, but reveal vast underwater facilities dedicated to the study of who-knows-what. Probably hidden for good reason.
You take an incoming hail. "Ho hooo, a visitor. A quiet one at that. Maybe here for quiet affairs? Then show proof."

choice.text: You do not have any RK Punk gear.

event.text: "... Slayer. Heheh. A drone will bring you the welcome gift. We await you."

choice.text: You do not have any RK gear at all.

event.text: "Yes yes... Let's do business. We are in need of a few things. We await you."
Your ship soon takes damage from a source you cannot identify. "Ha! Nice one. For us maybe. But you are very lost."

choice.text: We're just passing through here, don't mind us.

event.text:
There is no answer. You move a safe distance away and figure you have time to do some maintenance.
After some time, you detect some Orchid ships jumping in weapons hot, but you're safe here.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_PUNK_2
event.text:
"I take great pleasure in watching new workpieces arrive. Are you going to please me? HAH I'm just teasing you. But really, anything?"
You look at the list of "workpieces" in-demand and what you just heard only gets more creepy.

choice.text: You don't have Mantis for this (Uniques would rather kill you and themselves)

event.text:
"Reliable vigor, yes, a staple. Here's your check."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Potential vigor, yes, a staple. Here's your check."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Great potential, full of good molecules, yes! Here's a fat check."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Great potential, full of chemicals, yes! Here's a fat check."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Ho ho hooo, the rage, the vigor! Aren't you a sweetheart! Here's a huge check for your trouble."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

choice.text: Actually... we have an emergency, bye!

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_PUNK_3
event.text:
"Enough pleasantries, welcome to our best local store. I'm hearing voices... HAH gotcha, no, not like that. In my earpiece, sounds urgent. Be seeing you."
Before you shop, you still have some time to do something else aboard the ship.

event.name: RKS_LAB_PUNK_AMBUSH
event.text:
You barely even reach the Jump button as a small Free Mantis fleet jumps in! A very Warlord ship immediately hails you.
"Federation, federation. You our heroes! So WHY you here, doing evil trade??!" The already drugged-up Warlord looks devastated but is back on the warpath before you can even answer. "We come to kill them, but you with them! We kill you too!"
You wonder why they would come to people so adept at killing their kind (and everybody else too), but your scans reveal they came quite prepared!

choice.text: (Unique Mantis) TskKAK! Let pass! Secret mission.

event.text:
"So much vigor, so little intellect, and we have so. many. guns. Butler drone, tell the staff to get off their arses. And prepare my tea."
Projectiles from the RK Punk and the assault fleet start flying in every direction! Also, they must have used a special jammer to depower your jump drive, you will have to charge it again.

destroyed.text: The Elite Free Mantis ship breaks apart and you quickly salvage what you can before the rest of the fleet realizes what happened.

choice.text: Let's hope they remain such hardcore fans after this.

deadCrew.text: With the crew dead you quickly salvage what you can from the Elite Free Mantis ship before the rest of the fleet realizes what happened.

choice.text: Let's hope they remain such hardcore fans after this.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_SCORCH

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: You were told you can get great RadiKal Scorch gear at this beacon, if you can prove you're a customer.

event.text:
You arrive at a red planet with volcanoes so large you can see them from orbit, and around them, a multitude of structures that look like giant insects. The atmosphere looks like it's on fire. Taking a closer look, you notice that many of the "structures" actually move, sampling lava, putting things in it, and doing all sorts of strange experiments. You can also tell that there are many scientific facilities, factories and even what looks like stadiums all over the surface, a good distance away from the lava flows.
Someone on the planet is hailing you. "We see you, Federation Renegade. If you have reason to be here, now's time to show proof."

choice.text: You do not have any RK Scorch gear.

event.text: "... Brother. It always warms the heart to see one of us. Here is the welcome gift. You are cleared to land."

choice.text: You do not have any RK gear at all.

event.text: "Good. We've been awaiting some deliveries, and have quite the stockpile of equipment to sell. Let's trade then. You are cleared to land."
Your ship soon takes damage from a source you cannot identify. "Begone, tourist. You had been warned."

choice.text: We're just passing through here, don't mind us.

event.text:
There is no answer. You move a safe distance away and figure you have time to do some maintenance.
After some time, you detect some Orchid ships jumping in weapons hot, but you're safe here.

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_SCORCH_2
event.text:
"First, the deliveries. What raw materials do you bring? The furnaces, vivisection labs and distilleries hunger."
You look at the list of "materials" and get a better understanding of why this sector is outlawed by so many factions. However the mission comes first, right?

choice.text: You don't have Orchids for this (Uniques would rather kill you and themselves)

event.text:
"The base material. Always useful. Here's your payment."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"The base material. Always useful. Here's your payment."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Quality material, yes. Here's greater payment."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Quality material, yes. Here's greater payment."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Quality material, yes. Here's greater payment."
You can feel the rest of your crew judging you.

removeCrew.text: You cannot clone crew that you sold away to a painful death.

event.text:
"Fantastic! These are increasingly difficult to come by, as if the Orchids are tightening security over time. Here's your generous payment."
You can feel the rest of your crew looking at you in disgust, but nobody dares say anything.

choice.text: Actually... we have an emergency, bye!

event.name: RKS_QUEST_LAB_SCORCH_3
event.text:
"Alright, now enjoy our best local store. I'm getting some pressing reports that I have to look into. Pleasure doing business and all."
Before you shop, you still have some time to do something else aboard the ship.

event.name: RKS_LAB_SCORCH_AMBUSH
event.text:
You barely even reach the Jump button as a small Orchid fleet jumps in! A very angry Praetor ship immediately hails you.
"Federation. Scouts saw you engage with these people. You traitorous pest. We will avenge our people. Then raze this abomination of a planet."
You wonder why they would expose themselves to weaponry so specifically good against their biology, but your scans reveal they came quite prepared!

choice.text: (Unique Orchid) Let us go. Secret mission.
...To be honest, I expected something like this.

event.text:
"Goddamn Florals, why must you always try and stop progress? It's not our fault if these bodies make the best fuel! We didn't make the rules. Guards, it's time to do some gardening!"
Projectiles from the RK Scorch and the assault fleet start flying in every direction! Also, they must have used a special jammer to depower your jump drive, you will have to charge it again.

destroyed.text: The Elite Orchid ship breaks apart and you quickly salvage what you can before the rest of the fleet realizes what happened.

choice.text: Now we're killing Elite allies to defend arms dealers. Okay.

deadCrew.text: With the crew dead you quickly salvage what you can from the Elite Orchid ship before the rest of the fleet realizes what happened.

choice.text: Now we're killing Elite allies to defend arms dealers. Okay.

event.name: RKS_PIRATE_TRINITY

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The pirates await your scrap. Disappoint them in any way at your own risk!

event.text:
The pirate clans don't always see eye to eye, but when there's big money to be made, they know how to behave. The elite welcoming you here seems in charge of overseeing the transactions. "Sssso, you're the big shot Feddy cusssstomer who threw away a lawful life to mingle with ussss, eh? No offensssse but I've gotta tell you, I don't really buy it. We shall ssssee if you're for real..."
If you don't want to anger the slug and his ruthless associates, you had better buy something from each of the pirate subfactions. It's all-or-nothing, but if you do, pirates everywhere will respect you that much more!

choice.text: Yep that's me. Enough chitchat, show us the goods.

event.name: RKS_PIRATE_TRINITY_CHOOSE
event.text: The pirates are all looking at you expectantly.

choice.text: The slaver is counting their scrap, seated on a slave's face.
The mercenary is juggling bits of scrap, knives and loaded guns.
The smuggler is telling their third wife she can buy that Butler.
Thank them for the business and say goodbye.

event.text:
"Well well, you truly are one rich, ssssick individual with zero regard for other living thingsss, a fantasssstic customer by all accountssss! Accept my apologiesss for doubting you, and may your raidsss bring great bounty! Galsss and guyssss, IT'SSS PARTY TIME! Narcoticsss for everyone, and a round of Shoot-Tha-Limbs on that human sssslave who looked at me funny thisss morning!"
They let you leave with a little gift and big smiles on their faces. Congratulations, you have just made the galaxy a worse place.

choice.text: We won't actually do business with all of you. We're leaving.

event.name: RKS_PIRATE_TRINITY_CHOOSE_SLAVER
event.text: "From warships, mining camps, nurseries, outposts too, we bring best and brightest slaves! Sure one of them is your need... Unless you no wanna pay. Then we put slave collar on you too."
The slaver proudly shows off the poor souls for you to buy.
"Great buy. Sure they serve well, captain!"
"Great buy. Sure they serve well, captain!"
"Ah, I see you have eye for only best quality of slaves, captain. This is one of finest slaves ever. Sure they serve well, captain!"

choice.text: You know what, the funny thing is, we're broke...

event.text:
"Bad words feddy captain, should have choose better words. But mostly should have bring scrap." The pirate ship in front of you instantly makes itself combat ready, having clearly expected that.
You can't tell whether the slug overseer is furious, vindicated, or both. "Hah! I knew it, you just wanted to take down a bunch of piratesss at once you wannabe hero. Guesss what, we're not that dumb! Hey Kurt, hand over the scrap, i won the bet." With that, they and the other pirate ships jump away, leaving you to deal with the slaver!

event.name: RKS_PIRATE_TRINITY_CHOOSE_MERCENARY
event.text: "Stay one step ahead of your enemies with our intelligence services. From recon to distraction, all the way to more ...decisive action once we're acquainted well enough! Assuming you didn't make yourself our next target by coming here empty-handed, obviously."
The Syndicate mercenary awaits your orders.

choice.text: Buy a map of the sector.

event.text: "You got it, captain!"

choice.text: Delay the rebel fleet for 2 turns.

event.text: "We're on it, captain!"

choice.text: You know what, the funny thing is, we're broke...

event.text:
"Bad choice, captain. You should have brought that scrap." The pirate ship in front of you instantly makes itself combat ready, having clearly expected that.
You can't tell whether the slug overseer is furious, vindicated, or both. "Hah! I knew it, you just wanted to take down a bunch of piratesss at once you wannabe hero. Guesss what, we're not that dumb! Hey Kurt, hand over the scrap, i won the bet." With that, they and the other pirate ships jump away, leaving you to deal with the mercenary!

event.name: RKS_PIRATE_TRINITY_CHOOSE_SMUGGLER
event.text: "Do you know what separates good captains from great captains? Well, we do, and that's where they procure their weaponry. We provide those extra special weapons and augments that go beyond what you can find in your everyday store. And where did we get them? In the lifeless ships of non-paying captains, which you're too smart to be, right?"
The smuggler awaits your order.
"Enjoy that little bit of extra firepower!"
"Enjoy that extra firepower!"

choice.text: Buy a random very rare RadiKal augment.

event.text: "Use with caution!"

choice.text: You know what, the funny thing is, we're broke...

event.text:
"Sad, I didn't take you for a joker. You should have brought that scrap." The pirate ship in front of you instantly makes itself combat ready, having clearly expected that.
You can't tell whether the slug overseer is furious, vindicated, or both. "Hah! I knew it, you just wanted to take down a bunch of piratesss at once you wannabe hero. Guesss what, we're not that dumb! Hey Kurt, hand over the scrap, i won the bet." With that, they and the other pirate ships jump away, leaving you to deal with the smuggler!

event.name: RKS_PIRATE_TRINITY_FIGHT_CURRENT_PIRATE
event.text: "Of coursssse you are. Told you Kurt, hand over my sssscrap, you lost the bet. Let'ssss kill these damn clownssss." Looks like you'll have to fight your way out!

destroyed.text: The Slaver ship has been defeated. You scrap the remains. Intentionally or not, you've made a mess disrupting pirate operations in this sector.

deadCrew.text: The Slaver ship has been defeated. You scrap the interior and free a great slave from the cargo bay! Intentionally or not, you've made a mess disrupting pirate operations in this sector.

destroyed.text: The Mercenary ship has been defeated. You scrap the remains. Intentionally or not, you've made a mess disrupting pirate operations in this sector.

deadCrew.text: The Mercenary ship has been defeated. You scrap the interior and find detailed sector data in the terminal! Intentionally or not, you've made a mess disrupting pirate operations in this sector.

destroyed.text: The Smuggler ship has been defeated. You scrap the remains. Intentionally or not, you've made a mess disrupting pirate operations in this sector.

deadCrew.text: The Smuggler ship has been defeated. You scrap the interior and find a weapon in the cargo bay! Intentionally or not, you've made a mess disrupting pirate operations in this sector.
